meta.title: PicoMUTX — Get an AI Agent Running
meta.description: PicoMUTX helps teams install, configure, and run useful AI agents with guidance when setup gets complicated.

items.0: Start from zero, a stuck setup, or a live system
items.1: A clear setup path for install, repair, or review
items.2: Runs, approvals, and support before agents work unattended

problem.title: Most agent projects do not fail
problem.body: Projects stall when setup gets unclear: too many stack choices, too many keys, and no simple recovery path when something breaks.

0.label: "I know what I want the agent to do. I still need the fastest sane path to get one working."
0.body: Starting from zero is not the real problem. The real problem is choosing the stack, sequence, and standards without turning the work into endless research.

1.label: "I already built part of this. Now I need to recover the setup instead of restarting from scratch."
1.body: When setup breaks halfway through, you need the next repair step, not another research session.

2.label: "Something is already live, but I do not trust the cost, logic, or failure modes yet."
2.body: At that point it is not enough for the agent to exist. It has to be legible, controllable, and safe enough to touch real work.

3.label: "I need a system I can explain, review, and control before I rely on it."
3.body: If you cannot see runs, thresholds, approvals, and behavior under pressure, you do not have an operational asset yet.

problem.close: Pico turns that uncertainty into one practical next step.

platform.title: From stuck to shipped.
platform.body: Pico finds the blocker and gives you the next move.

0.title: Connect
0.body: Show Pico the goal, the agent, or the failure.

1.title: Fix
1.body: Get the exact next step—install, repair, or review.

2.title: Ship
2.body: Make the change and keep the context.

3.title: Escalate when the edge case is real
3.body: When setup needs API keys, hosting, integrations, or custom work, human guidance is available.

who.title: PicoMUTX is not for everyone.
who.notForYouTitle: This is not for you if…

forYou.0: You want to ship one useful agent without assembling the whole stack by guesswork
forYou.1: You already have a half-built or broken setup and need to recover momentum
forYou.2: You have something live or nearly live and need better review before it works unattended
forYou.3: You want outcome-first help, not a course or theory rabbit hole
forYou.4: You want a setup that gets easier to run over time

notForYou.0: You want a generic no-code toy that removes judgment from the work
notForYou.1: You already need enterprise governance across a mature agent fleet
notForYou.2: You want education, hype, or trend-chasing more than operational progress
notForYou.3: You expect AI to replace ownership, review, or accountability
notForYou.4: You only want consulting without using the product

beforeAfter.title: Before PicoMUTX vs. after

0.before: You are not sure whether to install, repair, or review the agent first
0.after: You get the next setup step for the actual blocker

1.before: Every issue sends you into a different tool, doc, or opinion loop
1.after: Install, recovery, and review stay in one product

2.before: A broken setup or vague runtime behavior kills momentum
2.after: The next action is visible and specific

3.before: You have an agent you cannot fully explain or review
3.after: You have something you can run, inspect, and improve

beforeAfter.close: The goal is not to learn AI in the abstract. The goal is to get something useful working and keep it maintainable.

pricingPage.stackTitle: What every plan is built on
pricingPage.stackBody: Academy lessons, stack guides, and product documentation stay available as Pico grows with your work.
pricingPage.finalBody: If the question is urgency, plan fit, or rollout shape, support should answer it directly and send you back into the right Pico route.

0.q: Do I need to know how to code?
0.a: No. Pico is built to make the next step clear without requiring an agent-engineering background.

1.q: What if I already tried and failed?
1.a: That is the point. Connect the stuck setup and start from the blocker.

2.q: What kind of agent work is this for?
2.a: Support flows, copilots, lead capture, follow-up, and other practical agent workflows.

3.a: Yes. The public waitlist is open again. Academy and product access are rolling out gradually.

4.a: PicoMUTX is the setup-focused entry point into MUTX. Heavier multi-team governance belongs in the broader MUTX stack.
